summary: Gruber reviews the Apple Watch Series 6, focusing on the new Graphite stainless steel finish and Solo Loop bands. He meticulously places Graphite on the spectrum of Apple's dark metallic finishes, concluding it sits between Space Black and regular silver — darker than silver but never approaching black. He praises the finish as a smart evolution that better suits the Series 4+ design language with its larger, rounded displays. The Solo Loop bands impress him with their lighter weight, suppleness, and precise sizing — he marvels that Apple correctly guessed his wrist size without asking. He expects both Solo Loop variants to become staples in his rotation.
key_points: Graphite stainless steel is distinctly lighter than Space Black and Space Gray iPhone finishes — it never reads as black, sitting at the lightest end of Apple's dark steel spectrum
Apple's product photography makes Graphite and Space Black look nearly identical, but in person the difference is obvious — words describe it better than photos
The Series 4+ design with larger rounded displays no longer needs Space Black's trick of disguising bezels, making the more obviously metallic Graphite a better fit
Solo Loops weigh half as much as Sport Bands and are dramatically more supple, feeling like a significant comfort upgrade
Apple somehow predicted reviewers' exact wrist sizes without asking, which Gruber finds genuinely uncanny
money_quotes: Apple's product photography is nonpareil, but there are some things you need to see in person.
The advantage, perhaps, of having boring but very consistent taste is that I'm very easy to predict.
I feel like I just lost $5 to a carnival barker who correctly guessed my age to the exact year, and I want to get back in line to bet another $5 to see if he can guess my weight.
Apple's Space Black DLC finish for stainless steel is truly jet black — it's the polished glossy black of Darth Vader's helmet.
It's more glanceably premium-looking. It's shiny.

If you hold a Sport Band by the connector and stick it out horizontally, it only droops a little, like a diving board in need of repair. If you hold one of the new Solo Loops by the connector, it droops straight down.
It's like baby's-butt-cheek soft and supple.
key_insight: Graphite stainless steel is a smarter choice than Space Black for modern Apple Watches because its lighter, mirror-like finish better complements the Series 4+ design while looking more obviously premium than the darkness that once served to hide large bezels.
